wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the QA Performance Testing Automation Project overview. Explain the project's purpose as a comprehensive Android application performance testing and build automation toolkit. Document the core functionality including APK build and modification utilities, performance monitoring system, CI/CD integration, and international distribution capabilities. Describe the target audience, key use cases, and system requirements. Include the project's architecture philosophy, design patterns, and relationship between major components. Provide an executive summary suitable for stakeholders and technical decision makers.

wiki_catalogs.name: APK Re-signing System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the APK re-signing system. Document the complete implementation of the againKey.py script including keystore type enumeration, configuration management, and command-line interface. Explain the secure APK signing process using Android SDK apksigner, multiple keystore support (SLP and RBP), and automatic verification workflow. Include practical examples of command-line usage, parameter configuration, and troubleshooting common signing issues. Address security considerations, keystore management best practices, and integration with build pipelines. Document the factory pattern used for keystore configuration and error handling strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Performance Data Collection
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the performance data collection system. Document the complete workflow from device connection via ADB to CPU usage monitoring with adaptive sampling. Explain the PckCpuInfo class for parsing top command output, the CpuCollector class for managing data collection threads, and the CpuMonitor class for orchestrating monitoring sessions. Detail the AndroidDevice interaction layer, SDK version compatibility handling, and the top command execution with different parameter variations. Include practical examples of CPU data collection, process parsing, and device interaction patterns. Document configuration options for collection intervals, timeout settings, and multi-package monitoring. Address troubleshooting collection issues and optimizing data accuracy.

wiki_catalogs.name: PGyer Platform Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for PGyer platform integration capabilities. Document the complete API configuration and authentication process including API key setup, install type configuration (public, password-protected, invitation-only), and security considerations. Explain the automated upload process from token acquisition through file upload to build information retrieval. Detail the callback mechanism for handling upload progress and completion status. Include practical examples of integrating with CI/CD pipelines, environment variable management for API credentials, and error handling strategies. Document the complete workflow from build completion detection to successful deployment notification. Address common integration challenges such as network connectivity issues, API rate limits, and authentication failures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Google Play Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Google Play integration system. Document service account configuration and OAuth2 authentication setup including client_secrets.json and androidpublisher.dat files. Explain the complete app bundle upload process using upload_apks_with_listing.py, including APK/AAB file handling, chunked uploads, and progress tracking. Detail the track management system covering alpha, beta, production, and rollout tracks. Document release notes translation automation and multi-language support. Include practical examples of service account setup, API authentication errors, and upload troubleshooting. Address security considerations, credential management, and access token refresh mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Overview and Design Philosophy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the system overview and design philosophy. Explain the modular CLI tool architecture with factory pattern implementation, describing how the system separates concerns across performance monitoring, build automation, and CI/CD integration layers. Document the design principles behind the configuration-driven approach, component isolation, and cross-platform compatibility. Include architectural diagrams showing the overall system layout, component boundaries, and data flow patterns. Address the rationale for choosing Python-based automation tools and shell script integration for cross-platform support.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API reference documentation covering all public interfaces and endpoints. Document command-line interfaces for all scripts including parameter specifications, return codes, and error handling. Detail Python module APIs including class hierarchies, method signatures, and usage patterns. Document configuration schemas for all components with default values and validation rules. Include HTTP API documentation for PGyer integration and Google Play Developer API endpoints. Provide concrete examples from the actual codebase showing proper usage patterns. Document authentication methods, rate limiting considerations, and error response formats. Include migration guides for deprecated features and backwards compatibility notes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for new users. Include detailed installation instructions, system requirements, and initial setup procedures. Document the basic workflow for performance testing and build automation. Provide step-by-step tutorials for common tasks including APK re-signing, channel package generation, and performance data collection. Include quick start examples with practical code snippets from the actual codebase. Address prerequisite knowledge requirements and provide troubleshooting guidance for common setup issues. Make content accessible to beginners while ensuring experienced developers can quickly get productive.

wiki_catalogs.name: Channel Package Generation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the channel packaging system using Walle integration. Document the batchChannelV2.py implementation for generating multiple APK variants with different channel identifiers. Explain the channel list management through changeChannelList.py, batch processing capabilities, and configuration file support. Include practical examples of channel naming strategies, batch operations, and output organization. Address Walle CLI integration, performance considerations for large-scale packaging, and troubleshooting channel packaging issues. Document the flexible channel configuration system and extension points for custom channel schemes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Performance Data Analysis
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for performance data analysis capabilities. Document the logcat integration for system-level logging during performance tests, startup time measurement algorithms in testPhoneTime.py, and utility functions for data processing. Explain statistical analysis techniques, data filtering methods, and anomaly detection patterns. Include examples of processing collected CSV data, calculating performance metrics, and identifying performance bottlenecks. Document the integration with external tools and the analysis workflow from raw data to actionable insights. Address data validation, error handling, and performance optimization techniques for large datasets.

wiki_catalogs.name: Cross-Platform Build Automation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for cross-platform build automation capabilities. Document Windows batch scripting implementation including the openPerf.bat script for performance testing workflows. Explain Linux/macOS shell script execution through run.sh and run.shell scripts. Cover environment variable management, platform-specific considerations, and path handling differences. Detail the complete build pipeline from initial setup through performance data collection to final artifact generation. Include practical examples of integrating these scripts into CI/CD workflows, parameter configuration, and output artifact management. Document troubleshooting approaches for platform-specific issues, permission problems, and dependency conflicts. Address performance optimization techniques and resource management across different operating systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Localization and Metadata Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for localization and metadata management system. Document the locale_package.sh script for generating localized packages across multiple regions and languages. Explain the GoogleTranslater class integration for automated content translation and the multi-language release note system supporting 18+ languages including English variants, Chinese dialects, Arabic, Indonesian, Korean, Malay, Thai, Turkish, and Vietnamese. Detail the metadata translation workflow, content validation, and character limit enforcement. Include practical examples of locale configuration, translation quality checks, and regional compliance requirements. Address localization best practices, content adaptation strategies, and automated translation workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Configuration Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the configuration management system. Thoroughly explain the centralized configuration class structure, parameter validation, and environment-specific settings management. Document configuration file formats, default values, and override mechanisms. Include concrete examples of how configuration parameters affect system behavior across different components. Explain the relationship between configuration and component initialization, error handling for invalid configurations, and dynamic configuration updates. Address configuration persistence, backup strategies, and migration between versions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Internal Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed internal architecture documentation describing the high-level design patterns and system boundaries. Document component interactions, data flows, and integration patterns throughout the codebase. Explain the Observer pattern implementation in performance monitoring, Factory pattern usage in keystore management, and Strategy pattern applications. Include architectural diagrams showing component relationships and data flow between modules. Document design decisions, trade-offs, and constraints that shaped the current architecture. Address cross-cutting concerns like logging, configuration management, and error handling. Provide system context diagrams and component breakdowns. Document technology stack choices, third-party dependencies, and version compatibility requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Systems Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the core systems. Describe the high-level design philosophy, modular CLI tool architecture with factory pattern implementation. Document component relationships, data flow between performance monitoring, build automation, and CI/CD integration layers. Explain the configuration management system, error handling mechanisms, and logging architecture. Include system boundaries, integration patterns, and technical decisions. Provide system context diagrams showing how APK build tools, performance monitoring, and distribution systems interact. Address cross-cutting concerns like security, monitoring, and scalability considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Resource Modification Tools
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for resource modification tools. Document the image processing capabilities in changeImage.py, APK structure modification through changeApk.py, and resource file manipulation in changeRes.py. Explain the implementation of batch image operations, APK asset extraction and replacement, and resource file validation. Include practical examples of common modification tasks like icon replacement, resource patching, and bulk asset updates. Address file format support, backup strategies, and integrity verification. Document the modular approach to resource modification and integration with the overall build pipeline.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Visualization and Reporting
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for data visualization and reporting capabilities. Document the CSV to chart conversion system, file selection and processing utilities, and chart generation functions. Explain supported chart types, customization options, and export formats. Include practical examples of generating performance charts from collected data, customizing visual representations, and creating automated reporting pipelines. Document the file processing workflow, chart styling options, and integration with external visualization libraries. Address common visualization challenges, data formatting requirements, and best practices for performance report creation.

wiki_catalogs.name: iOS Build and Distribution
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for iOS build and distribution workflows. Document the Flutter-based iOS build process using build_ipa.sh and build_ipa_223.sh scripts, including dependency management, certificate handling, and provisioning profile configuration. Explain the iOS uploader script (ios_uploader.sh) for App Store Connect integration and TestFlight distribution. Detail the build_app.sh script for general iOS application building. Include practical examples of iOS development environment setup, code signing configuration, and automated distribution pipelines. Address iOS-specific challenges including entitlements, app icons, splash screens, and platform-specific optimizations. Document troubleshooting for common iOS build and distribution issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Relationships and Data Flow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for component relationships and data flow patterns. Describe how performance monitoring, build automation, and CI/CD integration components interact through well-defined interfaces. Document the data flow between APK build tools, performance data collection, and distribution systems. Explain inter-component communication protocols, shared resources management, and dependency injection patterns. Include sequence diagrams showing typical workflows and error propagation mechanisms. Address component lifecycle management, resource cleanup, and graceful degradation scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Extension and Development Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive extension and development guidelines for customizing and extending the framework. Document plugin development patterns including how to create custom performance collectors by extending the Monitor base class. Explain how to implement additional channel packaging strategies and integrate new distribution platforms. Detail the configuration system extensibility and how to add new configuration parameters. Document best practices for maintaining compatibility with existing components. Include step-by-step tutorials for common extension scenarios like adding new performance metrics, customizing build processes, and integrating with additional CI/CD platforms. Provide code templates and examples for typical extension points. Address testing strategies for custom extensions and integration testing approaches.

wiki_catalogs.name: APK Build and Modification Tools
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for APK build and modification tools. Document the complete suite of utilities for APK re-signing, channel package generation, resource modification, and image processing. Explain the implementation details of each tool, command-line interfaces, and configuration options. Include practical examples demonstrating common use cases like batch channel packaging with Walle integration, APK re-signing with multiple keystores, and resource file manipulation. Address security considerations, keystore management, and troubleshooting common build issues. Provide guidance on extending functionality and customizing build processes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Application Information Extraction
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the application information extraction tool. Document the getAppInfo.py implementation for retrieving APK metadata, package information, and build details. Explain the AAPT (Android Asset Packaging Tool) integration, manifest parsing, and structured data extraction. Include practical examples of extracting version codes, package names, permissions, and other APK attributes. Address the tool's role in automated build processes, integration with CI/CD pipelines, and data validation. Document the output formats, error handling for malformed APKs, and extension possibilities for additional metadata extraction.

wiki_catalogs.name: Performance Testing Workflows
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document comprehensive performance testing workflows and automation. Explain cold start testing procedures, hot start testing methodologies, and multi-device performance comparison techniques. Detail the complete testing pipeline from test setup through data collection to result analysis. Include practical examples of configuring test scenarios, executing automated performance tests, and interpreting results. Document the integration with shell scripts for cross-platform execution, test environment setup, and result aggregation. Address test case design, performance benchmarking standards, and workflow optimization for continuous performance monitoring.

wiki_catalogs.name: Notification and Communication System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the notification and communication system used for international distribution updates. Explain the WeChat notification integration through wechat_notify.py for automated alerts during build and distribution processes. Detail the notification triggers, message formatting, recipient management, and integration with the broader distribution workflow. Include practical examples of notification configuration, webhook integration patterns, and custom notification handlers. Address notification delivery reliability, error handling, and fallback mechanisms. Document integration with other communication channels and monitoring systems for complete distribution visibility.

wiki_catalogs.name: Error Handling and Logging Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive content for error handling and logging architecture. Document the logging framework implementation, log levels, and structured logging patterns used throughout the system. Explain error propagation mechanisms, exception handling strategies, and recovery procedures. Include examples of common error scenarios and their resolution patterns. Document log aggregation, retention policies, and diagnostic information collection. Address debugging workflows, performance impact of logging, and production monitoring considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Contributing Guidelines
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed contributing guidelines for developers wanting to contribute to the project. Document code style standards and formatting requirements consistent with the existing codebase. Specify testing requirements including unit tests, integration tests, and performance tests. Outline the pull request process including review criteria, branch naming conventions, and merge requirements. Document issue reporting procedures and bug fix workflows. Include guidelines for documentation updates and example contributions. Address versioning strategy, release processes, and backward compatibility requirements. Provide setup instructions for development environments and testing frameworks. Document community standards and communication guidelines for contributors.

wiki_catalogs.name: Performance Monitoring System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the performance monitoring system. Document the complete workflow from device connection via ADB to performance data collection, analysis, and visualization. Explain CPU usage monitoring with adaptive sampling, memory analysis, FPS collection, and temperature monitoring. Include the device interaction layer, data aggregation and filtering, statistical analysis, and chart generation capabilities. Document performance testing workflows including cold start and hot start testing. Provide practical examples of data collection, analysis techniques, and report generation. Address troubleshooting performance monitoring issues and optimizing data collection processes.

wiki_catalogs.name: CI/CD Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for CI/CD integration capabilities. Document the PGyer platform integration including API configuration, authentication, and automated upload processes. Explain cross-platform build automation with Windows batch scripting and Linux/macOS shell scripts. Include environment variable management, build artifact handling, and deployment pipeline integration. Document the complete workflow from build completion detection through performance data collection to API communication and result reporting. Provide practical examples of integrating with Jenkins and other CI/CD platforms. Address common integration challenges and troubleshooting guidance.

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the international distribution system. Document Google Play integration including service account configuration, API authentication, and app bundle upload processes. Explain localization and metadata management for multi-language support and regional requirements. Include iOS build and distribution workflows with Flutter integration, IPA generation, and App Store Connect processes. Document the complete internationalization pipeline from localization package generation to automated uploads. Address regional compliance requirements, content delivery optimization, and notification systems. Provide practical examples and troubleshooting guidance for international distribution challenges.

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive troubleshooting and maintenance documentation. Include migration guides for system updates and compatibility considerations. Address scalability planning and system health monitoring.

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive developer reference documentation. Document the complete API reference including command-line interfaces, Python module documentation, and configuration schemas. Include detailed parameter specifications, return codes, and error messages. Document internal architecture patterns, component interactions, and data flow diagrams. Document design patterns used throughout the codebase and best practices for extending functionality.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# QA Performance Testing Automation Project

## Project Introduction

This project is a comprehensive Android application performance testing and build automation toolkit designed for QA engineers and development teams. The project provides automated solutions for APK building, signing, channel packaging, performance monitoring, and CI/CD integration.

### Purpose Statement
The project aims to streamline the Android application testing workflow by providing automated tools for:
- APK build and modification operations
- Performance data collection and analysis
- Channel package generation for different distribution platforms
- CI/CD pipeline integration for automated deployments

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Automate Build Processes**: Reduce manual effort in APK creation and modification
- **Performance Monitoring**: Provide comprehensive performance metrics collection
- **Channel Management**: Enable efficient multi-channel APK generation
- **CI/CD Integration**: Support automated deployment pipelines
- **Cross-Platform Support**: Handle both domestic and international distribution channels

### Target Audience
- QA Engineers conducting performance testing
- Development Teams requiring automated build processes
- DevOps Engineers setting up CI/CD pipelines
- Mobile App Developers needing performance analysis tools

## Technical Architecture

The project follows a modular architecture with clear separation of concerns across different functional domains:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[QA PerfCode Project] --> B[App Build Tools]
    A --> C[Mobile Performance]
    A --> D[CI/CD Integration]
    A --> E[Overseas Build]
    
    B --> B1[APK Signing]
    B --> B2[Channel Packaging]
    B --> B3[Resource Modification]
    
    C --> C1[Performance Collection]
    C --> C2[Data Analysis]
    C --> C3[Report Generation]
    
    D --> D1[PGyer Upload]
    D --> D2[Automation Scripts]
    
    E --> E1[Google Play Upload]
    E --> E2[Localization]
    E --> E3[Multi-region Support]
```

### Component Breakdown

**Core Modules:**
- **appBuild**: APK build and modification utilities
- **mobilePerf**: Performance monitoring and data analysis
- **ciBuild**: Continuous Integration and deployment tools
- **overseaBuild**: International distribution platform integration

**Design Patterns:**
- **Factory Pattern**: Used in APK signing configuration management
- **Strategy Pattern**: Applied in different build and upload strategies
- **Observer Pattern**: Implemented in performance monitoring systems
- **Command Pattern**: Utilized in CLI tool implementations

### System Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant QA as QA Engineer
    participant Build as App Build Tools
    participant Perf as Performance Monitor
    participant CI as CI/CD Pipeline
    participant Store as Distribution Store
    
    QA->>+Build: Request APK Build
    Build->>+Perf: Collect Performance Data
    Perf->>+CI: Upload Results
    CI->>+Store: Deploy to Store
    Store-->>-CI: Deployment Status
    CI-->>-Perf: Performance Metrics
    Perf-->>-Build: Test Results
    Build-->>-QA: Completed APK
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Application Build Tools**
- `appBuild/openBuild.bat` - Windows batch interface for build operations
- `appBuild/DaBao/batchChannelV2.py` - Channel package generation
- `appBuild/againBuild/againKey.py` - APK re-signing functionality

**Performance Monitoring**
- `mobilePerf/run.sh` - Linux/macOS performance data processing script
- `mobilePerf/tools/testPhoneTime.py` - Application startup time measurement
- `mobilePerf/perfCode/cpu_top.py` - CPU performance monitoring

**CI/CD Integration**
- `ciBuild/utils/upload_pgyer.py` - PGyer platform upload automation
- `ciBuild/sh_pgyer_upload.sh` - Shell script for upload operations

**International Distribution**
- `overseaBuild/upload_gp/upload_apks_with_listing.py` - Google Play upload automation
- `overseaBuild/build_ipa.sh` - iOS build automation

### Core Modules

**Configuration Management**
- `mobilePerf/perfCode/common/config.py` - Centralized configuration settings
- `mobilePerf/perfCode/common/utils.py` - Utility functions and helpers

**Performance Data Collection**
- `mobilePerf/perfCode/androidDevice.py` - Android device interaction layer
- `mobilePerf/perfCode/globaldata.py` - Global runtime data management
- `mobilePerf/perfCode/logcat.py` - Logcat data extraction

**Build Process Management**
- `appBuild/changeImage.py` - Image processing utilities
- `appBuild/DaBao/getAppInfo.py` - APK information extraction

### External Dependencies

**Performance Monitoring Dependencies:**
- `matplotlib` - Data visualization library
- `numpy` - Numerical computing support
- `pandas` - Data manipulation and analysis
- `requests` - HTTP client for API communications

**Build and Automation Dependencies:**
- `subprocess` - Process execution for system commands
- `argparse` - Command-line argument parsing
- `pathlib` - Path manipulation utilities

**International Distribution Dependencies:**
- `google-api-python-client` - Google Play Developer API client
- `oauth2client` - OAuth authentication support

### Integration Points

**ADB Integration**: Direct Android Debug Bridge communication for device control
**API Integration**: PGyer and Google Play APIs for automated uploads
**File System Integration**: CSV and image file processing for data export
**Network Integration**: HTTP requests for remote service communication

### Component Relationships

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[CLI Tools] --> B[Core Functions]
    B --> C[Data Processing]
    C --> D[Output Generation]
    
    A --> E[Configuration]
    E --> F[Environment Setup]
    
    B --> G[External Services]
    G --> H[PGyer API]
    G --> I[Google Play API]
    G --> J[ADB Commands]
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**APK Build and Modification**
- Automated APK re-signing with multiple keystore support
- Batch channel package generation using Walle tool
- Resource modification capabilities for app customization
- Image processing utilities for asset management

**Performance Monitoring**
- Real-time CPU, memory, and FPS data collection
- Application startup time measurement (cold/hot start)
- Automated performance data visualization
- Multi-device performance comparison

**CI/CD Integration**
- Automated PGyer upload after successful builds
- Cross-platform build automation scripts
- Environment-specific configuration management
- Deployment pipeline integration

**International Distribution**
- Google Play Console automation
- Multi-language metadata translation
- Regional localization support
- Multi-region distribution management

### Implementation Highlights

**Performance Data Collection Pipeline**
- `mobilePerf/perfCode/cpu_top.py` - CPU usage monitoring with adaptive sampling
- `mobilePerf/tools/testPhoneTime.py` - Startup time measurement with statistical analysis
- `mobilePerf/tools/csvToChart.py` - Automated data visualization generation

**Build Automation System**
- `appBuild/DaBao/batchChannelV2.py` - Flexible channel package generation with multiple input formats
- `appBuild/againBuild/againKey.py` - Secure APK signing with configurable keystore management
- `ciBuild/utils/upload_pgyer.py` - Robust upload mechanism with retry logic and progress tracking

**International Distribution Management**
- `overseaBuild/upload_gp/upload_apks_with_listing.py` - Comprehensive Google Play upload with metadata translation
- `overseaBuild/locale_package.sh` - Localization package generation for different regions

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> BuildPreparation
    BuildPreparation --> APKGeneration: Build Config Ready
    APKGeneration --> Signing: APK Created
    Signing --> ChannelPackaging: APK Signed
    ChannelPackaging --> PerformanceTesting: Channels Generated
    PerformanceTesting --> ReportGeneration: Tests Complete
    ReportGeneration --> Deployment: Reports Ready
    Deployment --> [*]: Process Complete
    
    PerformanceTesting --> PerformanceTesting: Data Collection Loop
    PerformanceTesting --> ReportGeneration: Collection Stopped
```

**Sources:**
- [README.md](README.md)
- [batchChannelV2.py](appBuild/DaBao/batchChannelV2.py)
- [againKey.py](appBuild/againBuild/againKey.py)
- [config.py](mobilePerf/perfCode/common/config.py)
- [testPhoneTime.py](mobilePerf/tools/testPhoneTime.py)
- [upload_pgyer.py](ciBuild/utils/upload_pgyer.py)
- [upload_apks_with_listing.py](overseaBuild/upload_gp/upload_apks_with_listing.py)
- [cpu_top.py](mobilePerf/perfCode/cpu_top.py)
- [run.sh](mobilePerf/run.sh)
- [openBuild.bat](appBuild/openBuild.bat)

</blog>
